title: Aureline operational incident workspace
description: Boundary schema for the incident_workspace_record frozen in docs/ops/incident_workspace_contract.md. One record family pins the operational incident a responder works inside: the incident header (incident_id, title, summary, operational severity, lifecycle state, ownership), the pinned code-context the responder pinned at incident open (exact-build identity, branch / commit handles, code-pointer refs), the alert / page snapshot the incident was opened against (alert source, alert id, captured-at, freshness), the typed log / trace / metric signal slices with provenance (live capture, mirror imported, offline replay, snapshot imported), the bounded resource snapshot (queue depths, handle counts, governor state) the responder observed, the runbook-packet refs the workspace is pointing at, the action-ledger entry refs recorded against the workspace, the browser / console handoff packet refs every attributable transition out of the local control plane travels through, and the export-bundle manifest refs the workspace pinned for postmortem / review. The workspace is read-mostly by default: every mutating action lands as an action-ledger entry that inherits the same execution-context / workspace-trust / approval-ticket / break-glass posture as ordinary runtime actions; mutating_action_admission_class fails closed at mutating_admission_unknown_pending_review when the responder cannot resolve the posture. The record never carries raw provider URLs, raw provider payloads, raw stdout / stderr, raw stack frames, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw response bodies, raw alert payloads, raw operator identity strings, raw approval-ticket bodies, raw browser-cookie material, or raw secret material; rows carry opaque ids, typed vocabulary, redaction-aware reviewable labels (<= 1024 graphemes), and freshness / lifecycle / target-context / handoff tokens only. Composes with (and does not replace) ADR-0007 (secret-broker handle / raw-secret-forbidden boundary), ADR-0009 (execution-context and scope), ADR-0010 (browser-handoff and approval-ticket envelope), ADR-0018 (workspace-trust state), schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json (target identity / sandbox posture / policy epoch), schemas/runtime/forensic_packet.schema.json (resource counters / strike windows / heartbeat / fault-domain / host-class lineage the snapshot composes with by reference), schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json (browser-handoff packet envelope), schemas/integration/approval_ticket.schema.json (approval-ticket envelope), schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json (security-side private-triage packet the operational workspace composes with by reference), schemas/security/break_glass_event.schema.json (break-glass event the action-ledger may invoke), schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json (emergency-action / revocation / disable-bundle linkage), schemas/support/support_bundle.schema.json (support / export bundle the workspace may compose with by reference), schemas/ops/runbook_packet.schema.json (runbook-packet / action-ledger entry contract), and schemas/ops/evidence_handoff_bundle.schema.json (immutable-snapshot bundle the workspace exports for postmortem / review / browser-handoff / console-handoff / last-synced-offline-view consumption). The eventual ops crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every incident-workspace render, support / export reader, hosted-review reader, postmortem / review reader, AI-tool review surface, browser-handoff exit, console-handoff exit, last-synced offline view reader, and admin / policy review reads. Adding a new record_kind, operational_severity_class, lifecycle_state_class, alert_source_class, signal_slice_kind_class, signal_origin_provenance_class, mutating_action_admission_class, denial_reason, or audit_event_id is additive-minor and bumps incident_workspace_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

incident_workspace_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the operational incident-workspace vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Always incident_workspace_record for workspaces conforming to this schema. Distinct from schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json (security-side private-triage packet).

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, journal entries, support bundles, postmortem / review bundles, and admin reconciliation surfaces. Raw provider URLs, raw provider payloads, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw response bodies, raw alert payloads, raw operator identity strings, raw approval-ticket bodies, raw browser-cookie material, and raw secret material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary safe for incident-workspace render, postmortem / review, support / export, browser / console handoff exit, last-synced offline view, and admin reconciliation surfaces; never carries the raw payload.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in preview, postmortem / review, and export review surfaces. Never carries the raw payload.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class re-exported from ADR-0010 / ADR-0007. Raw provider payloads, raw stack frames, raw stdout / stderr, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw response bodies, raw alert payloads, raw operator identity strings, raw approval-ticket bodies, raw browser-cookie material, and raw secret material never cross this boundary regardless of class. private_triage_only is admissible only when the workspace is bound to a security-side incident_workspace_packet record.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0018 / ADR-0009 workspace-trust state the workspace was admitted under.

policy_context.description: Policy-context envelope re-exported from ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 / ADR-0018.

operational_severity_class.description: Frozen operational-severity vocabulary. Distinct from the security-severity vocabulary in schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json. operational_severity.unknown_pending_review is the safe-default that strips mutating_admitted_under_* postures.

lifecycle_state_class.description: Frozen lifecycle-state vocabulary. imported_replay_no_live_lifecycle is admissible only on workspaces opened from an imported replay bundle. lifecycle_state_unknown_requires_review is the safe-default.

alert_source_class.description: Frozen alert-source vocabulary. Names what opened the incident workspace. manual_responder_open_no_alert covers responder-initiated opens that do not have a paging-provider alert behind them. imported_alert_no_live_source is the offline / replay-bundle case.

alert_freshness_class.description: Frozen alert-freshness vocabulary. Mirrors the freshness vocabulary in schemas/work_items/work_item_detail.schema.json so a workspace cannot paint a stale alert as fresh.

signal_slice_kind_class.description: Frozen signal-slice-kind vocabulary. Names what category of signal the slice carries.

signal_origin_provenance_class.description: Frozen signal-origin-provenance vocabulary. Names where the slice came from. ai_tool_proposed_slice_pending_review forbids leaving pending review state.

signal_embedding_class.description: Frozen signal-embedding vocabulary. embedded_after_redaction is admissible only when redaction_pass_class names the matching redaction pass; raw payload bytes never cross this boundary.

redaction_pass_class.description: Frozen redaction-pass vocabulary. broker_default_* values inherit the redaction defaults frozen in schemas/security/secret_class_rows.schema.json (ADR-0007). no_redaction_metadata_only is admissible only when signal_embedding_class is embedded_metadata_only_no_payload.

mutating_action_admission_class.description: Frozen mutating-action admission vocabulary. The workspace is read-mostly by default: read_mostly_default_no_mutation_admitted forbids any mutating action-ledger entry. Mutating actions inherit the same sandbox / approval posture as ordinary runtime actions; mutating_admission_unknown_pending_review is the safe-default that strips every admissible_under_* posture.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event vocabulary for incident-workspace surfaces.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary. Apply paths cite a typed reason rather than describing the cause in prose. Closed list; new values are additive-minor.

incident_commander_actor_ref.description: Opaque pseudonymous ref to the incident commander. Raw operator identity strings never appear here.

communications_lead_actor_ref.description: Opaque pseudonymous ref to the communications lead. Null when the workspace has not assigned a communications lead.

scribe_actor_ref.description: Opaque pseudonymous ref to the scribe. Null when the workspace has not assigned a scribe.

on_call_responder_actor_refs.description: Opaque pseudonymous refs to the on-call responders attached to the workspace.

ownership_envelope.description: Ownership envelope. Raw operator identity strings never appear; pseudonymous actor refs only.

exact_build_identity_refs.description: Opaque refs into schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json. Free-text 'version 1.x' is non-conforming.

branch_handle_refs.description: Opaque branch handles. Raw branch / commit URLs never appear.

commit_handle_refs.description: Opaque commit handles. Raw commit URLs never appear.

code_pointer_refs.description: Opaque refs to code-pointer records. Raw absolute paths never appear.

pinned_code_context.description: Pinned code context. Pinned at workspace open and re-pinned on lifecycle changes; the pinned values are preserved verbatim into the evidence-handoff bundle so postmortem / review readers resolve the same code identity the responder saw.

alert_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the upstream alert / page record. Null only when alert_source_class is manual_responder_open_no_alert or alert_source_unverifiable_user_review_required.

alert_page_snapshot.description: Alert / page snapshot envelope. Raw alert payload bytes never cross this boundary; the snapshot carries the alert source class, the opaque alert id, the freshness class, the capture timestamp, and a redaction-safe summary only.

embedded_payload_ref.description: Opaque ref to the embedded payload. Non-null only when signal_embedding_class is embedded_after_redaction or embedded_metadata_only_no_payload. Raw payload bytes never appear; the ref resolves through the signal-slice resolver under the redaction envelope named on redaction_pass_class.

reference_ref.description: Opaque ref to the artefact that carries the body when signal_embedding_class is by_reference_only. Null otherwise.

allOf.description: signal_embedding_class = embedded_after_redaction MUST cite a non-null embedded_payload_ref and a non-null redaction_pass_class outside no_redaction_metadata_only.
signal_embedding_class = by_reference_only MUST cite a non-null reference_ref and forbid embedded_payload_ref.
signal_embedding_class = omitted_redaction_required forbids embedded_payload_ref and reference_ref.
signal_embedding_class = embedded_metadata_only_no_payload pairs with redaction_pass_class = no_redaction_metadata_only.

signal_slice.description: One log / trace / metric / event / synthetic-check / audit-log slice attached to the workspace. Raw payload bytes never cross this boundary; the slice carries an embedding class, an opaque ref to the body (embedded or by-reference), the redaction-pass class that ran before the body was embedded, and a redaction-safe summary.

linked_forensic_packet_refs.description: Opaque refs into schemas/runtime/forensic_packet.schema.json. The detailed resource counters (CPU percent, memory, queue depths, handle counts, strike windows, heartbeat, fault-domain) live on the forensic packet; the workspace pins the ref rather than re-deriving the counters.

resource_snapshot.description: Resource-snapshot envelope. The detailed counters live on the linked forensic packet; the workspace pins the ref and a redaction-safe summary so the responder reads one structured snapshot row instead of inventing a parallel set of counters.

handoff_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the browser-handoff packet (schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json) or console-handoff session record.

handoff_kind_class.description: Frozen handoff-kind vocabulary. Names which attributable transition the workspace is recording.

browser_or_console_handoff_record_ref.description: One browser / console handoff transition record. Every attributable exit out of the local control plane travels through this ref so the transition is visible in the chronology rather than a hidden alternate control plane.

incident_id.description: Opaque, stable id for the operational incident. Stable across the workspace's life and never rewritten on lifecycle change.

signal_slices.description: Log / trace / metric / event / synthetic-check / audit-log slices attached to the workspace. Each slice carries its own embedding state and redaction-pass class.

runbook_packet_id_refs.description: Opaque refs to schemas/ops/runbook_packet.schema.json runbook_packet_record ids the workspace is pointing at.

action_ledger_entry_id_refs.description: Opaque refs to schemas/ops/runbook_packet.schema.json action_ledger_entry_record ids recorded against this workspace. Append-only; corrections supersede via supersedes_action_ledger_entry_ref on the entry, not by editing this list in place.

browser_or_console_handoff_records.description: Browser / console handoff records attributing every transition out of the local control plane. Empty when the workspace has not handed off to a browser or console.

evidence_handoff_bundle_id_refs.description: Opaque refs to schemas/ops/evidence_handoff_bundle.schema.json bundle ids the workspace exported (postmortem / review / browser-handoff / console-handoff / last-synced-offline-view). The export-bundle manifest is the closed list of bundles the workspace has pinned for review; corrections supersede via a new bundle, not by rewriting this list in place.

linked_security_incident_packet_refs.description: Opaque refs to schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json (security-side private-triage packet). Non-empty when the operational incident also opened a security triage.

linked_break_glass_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to schemas/security/break_glass_event.schema.json. Non-empty when the workspace's action-ledger invoked a break-glass profile.

resolved_at.description: Wall-clock at which the workspace's lifecycle entered resolved_postmortem_pending or resolved_postmortem_published. Null otherwise.

closed_at.description: Wall-clock at which the workspace's lifecycle entered closed_no_postmortem_required or withdrawn_duplicate_or_invalid. Null otherwise.

allOf.description: read_mostly_default_no_mutation_admitted forbids the workspace from referencing action-ledger entries on mutating intents; downstream validators check the action-ledger.step_intent_class via the ref. The workspace itself does not embed action-ledger bodies.
lifecycle_state_class = imported_replay_no_live_lifecycle MUST resolve mutating_action_admission_class to read_mostly_default_no_mutation_admitted (no live mutation against an imported replay).
alert_source_class = imported_alert_no_live_source MUST pair with lifecycle_state_class = imported_replay_no_live_lifecycle.
lifecycle_state_class = resolved_postmortem_pending or resolved_postmortem_published MUST cite a non-null resolved_at.
lifecycle_state_class = closed_no_postmortem_required or withdrawn_duplicate_or_invalid MUST cite a non-null closed_at.

incident_workspace_record.description: One operational incident workspace record. Read-mostly by default; mutating actions inherit the same sandbox / approval posture as ordinary runtime actions and land as action-ledger entries that supersede prior entries via stable id rather than rewriting them in place. Browser / console handoffs travel through browser_or_console_handoff_records so every attributable transition out of the local control plane is visible in the chronology. Postmortem / review / browser-handoff / console-handoff / last-synced-offline-view exports travel through evidence_handoff_bundle_id_refs; corrections supersede via a new bundle rather than rewriting the prior bundle in place.
